items.title: Arab leaders endorse Egypt's postwar plan for Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Egyptian President Abdel Fattah el-Sissi says Arab leaders have endorsed Egypt's postwar plan for the Gaza Strip.

Speaking at a summit of Arab nations in Cairo, Al-Sissi said the reconstruction plan would allow Gaza's roughly two million Palestinians to remain in the territory. The leaders called on the international community to support the plan. Their goal is to counter Donald Trump's plan to quote "take over" Gaza and resettle Palestinians in neighboring countries.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: What will Trump implement next from "Project 2025"? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>President Trump has moved quickly in his first month-and-a-half in office, with a barrage of executive orders, a severe cutback of the federal workforce,  and cuts to foreign aid. Much of what he has done was foreshadowed in a document written by conservatives titled Project 2025, which was a 'blueprint' for how Trump should govern in his second term. So, the question now is... what else might we expect?

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Europe to massivly boost defense spending, Ukraine support | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>European Union commission president Ursula von der Leyen says Europe is ready to massively boost its defense spending and take responsibility for European security. She has proposed a five-part plan to mobilize some  800 billion euros for defense – and pledged "immediate" military support for Ukraine after the US paused aid to the country.

items.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine ready to work under Trump's 'strong leadership' | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says his country is ready to work under Donald Trump's 'strong leadership'. Zelenskyy also called for a 'truce', and said Kyiv is ready to sign a deal with the US to extract minerals from Ukrainian soil.  

Zelenskyy's comments came after the US suspended all military aid for Ukraine's defense against Russia. Washington said it was pausing and reviewing support to ensure it 'contributes to a solution'.

items.title: Arab leaders meet to counter Trump's plan for Gaza | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Leaders from Arab nations and international organisations have gathered in Egypt for a summit on Gaza's reconstruction. 

Their aim is to counter Donald Trump's plan to quote "take over" Gaza and resettle Palestinians to neighboring countries. Arab leaders are calling on the international community for support. In his opening address, summit host President Abdel Fattah al-Sisi said his government's proposal meant Palestinians would "remain on their land" and the territory would be run by a committee of Palestinian technocrats.

00:00 Egypt's plan for Gaza
00:35 Karim El Gawhary, Journalist

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Harrowing reports of sexual violence against children in Sudan's civil war | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>WARNING: Distressing content of sexual violence

The UN's children's agency, UNICEF, has released a harrowing report on sexual violence in Sudan's civil war. It says armed men are raping and sexually assaulting children, including infants. The report has compiled more than 200 cases over the last 14 months, but says the figures represent only a small fraction of incidents.   

00:00 Reports of sexual violence against children
02:33 Eva Hinds, Unicef Chief of Communications

items.title: What US military aid pause means for Ukraine's future | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump has paused military aid to Ukraine, marking a dramatic shift in US policy. If the suspension holds long-term, the effect on the battlefield could be dramatic. The United States is by far the largest supplier of military aid to Ukraine, though when total aid, including humanitarian assistance is factored in, Europe spends marginally more. The decision comes after a disastrous meeting between US President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy last week where the two leaders had been supposed to sign a minerals deal which would have opened up Ukraine's natural resources to American investors. Before the news of the military aid pause was announced, DW Business spoke to Pavlo Verkhniatskyi, Managing Director at COSA solutions, a consulting firm focused on technological development - founded shortly after Russia’s annexation of Crimea in 2014.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Bully diplomacy: Is Trump forcing Ukraine into a peace deal with Russia? | DW News Desk
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>With US support to Ukraine in question, can Europe fill the gap, and how long can Kyiv hold out? The Trump administration has paused military aid to Ukraine, ramping up pressure on President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to negotiate peace with Russia. The move follows a tense Oval Office meeting where Trump and Vice President JD Vance scolded Zelenskyy for lacking gratitude after receiving $180 billion in US aid. Trump insists Ukraine must show a “commitment” to ending the war before funding resumes. Critics see the freeze as political extortion that could embolden Russia.  

#newsdesk #DonaldTrump #Ukraine
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Live: US President Trump addresses joint session of Congress | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump delivers his address to a joint session of Congress on Monday. He was expected to continue to outline his administration's priorities. Trump's address is technically not considered a State of the Union Address, as those are usually given following at least one year in office to reflect on the past year and plans ahead. The address is followed by a rebuttal from the opposite party, this year Democrat Senator Elissa Slotkin of Michigan.

#TrumpSpeech #USCongress #TrumpAddress

items.title: Markets tumble as US slaps tariffs on Canada, Mexico | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump said on Monday that Washington would apply previously delayed tariffs against Mexico and Canada. US stocks closed sharply lower in afternoon trading following Trump's comments. Besides the levies on its two neighbors, Trump also imposed 10% tariffs on Chinese products in February. The rate is set to rise to 20% on Tuesday. Washington's measures have sparked fears of a trade war in deeply integrated economies in North America.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Could peace plan shift focus of Ukraine peace efforts from US to Europe? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Details of the potential European-led peace plan for Ukraine remain vague, but British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Sunday that leaders had agreed on four points: 
1. The continuation of military aid to Ukraine and economic pressure on Russia.  
2. Any peace deal must guarantee Ukraine's sovereignty and security. And Ukraine must be at the table. 
3. In peacetime, Ukraine's allies will continue to strengthen Ukraine's defenses to deter future aggression. 
4. A so-called "coalition of the willing" will be formed to defend the peace agreement.

Chapters:
00:00 Europe leads new push for peace in Ukraine
01:30 Margus Tsahkna, Estonian Foreign Minister
08:54 Trump still to back Europe's plan for Ukraine
10:01 Stephen Wertheim, Carnegie Endowment for International Peace

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: No all-clear for Pope Francis' health | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Pope Francis has been hospitalized for 18 days and has not been seen since. Francis, who had part of his right lung removed as a young man, is currently undergoing oxygen therapy and breathing exercises. Complex respiratory infections and bilateral pneumonia make this necessary.

Chapters:
00:00 Pope suffers 'acute respiratory insufficiency'
00:35 Alina Trabbattoni, Journalist

items.title: How might this affect Ukraine on the battlefield in the short term? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>U.S. President Donald Trump has suspended all military aid to Ukraine, following a dispute with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House last week.
The halt comes after Trump accused Zelenskyy of being uninterested in peace and ungrateful for U.S. support. White House officials said U.S. aid would be reviewed to ensure it was "contributing to a solution. 

Chapters:
